claims.claim: Putrajaya's 4th Harmony Bill draft cites Singapore, Australia, Zimbabwe.
claims.source: The Star, 7 Apr 2026; Malay Mail/Bernama, 7 Apr 2026
claims.correction: Comparator countries verified. '4th draft' is broadly supportable only if the three 2015 NUCC bills are counted together with the 2026 redraft. Phrase as 'a renewed attempt after three 2015 NUCC draft bills' rather than standalone mathematical certainty.
claims.stale_check: Current as of 17 May 2026. The 12 May 2026 ministry clarification says the proposal remains in engagement and may be discontinued.

claims.claim: Bernama, 7 April 2026, 'Bill to set up Harmony and Reconciliation Commission expected to be tabled this year': https://www.bernama.com/en/general/news.php?id=1814136
claims.source: Bernama page opened at https://www.bernama.com/en/general/news.php?id=1814136 shows the article date as 15/02/2020 and refers to Senator P. Waytha Moorthy, not Aaron Ago Dagang.
claims.correction: Do not cite this URL as a 7 April 2026 Bernama article. It is a 15 February 2020 Bernama article about Waytha Moorthy. Use the 7 April 2026 Malay Mail/Bernama article or The Star article for the 2026 announcement.

claims.claim: It is the fourth draft in 14 years.
claims.correction: 14-year frame plausible from Najib's 2012 pledge to 2026. 'Fourth draft' count is less clean because Aaron's statement says NUCC drafted three bills in 2015, including one commission bill. Rephrase: 'It is the latest attempt in a 14-year reform cycle, after three NUCC draft bills in 2015.'

claims.claim: Anwar pledged the same in 2018 and 2022.
claims.source: Pakatan Harapan 2018 manifesto page 62; IBAHRI joint letter, 31 May 2023
claims.correction: The 2018 pledge was a Pakatan Harapan manifesto pledge, not a verified personal pledge by Anwar. Rephrase: 'Pakatan Harapan's 2018 manifesto pledged repeal; rights groups reported that PH's 2022 manifesto again promised to review and repeal provisions of laws such as the Sedition Act.'
claims.stale_check: Attribution to Anwar personally is over-strong.

claims.claim: CIJ recorded 36 sedition cases in 2025, an 84% rise from the year before.
claims.source: Daily Express republishing FMT report, 10 Dec 2025, https://www.dailyexpress.com.my/news/272227/sedition-act-cases-up-84-in-2025-says-cij
claims.correction: CIJ reportedly said 36 cases in 2025 versus 19 in 2024. But 36 versus 19 is approximately an 89.5% increase, not 84%. Could not locate the CIJ 2025 primary report. Rephrase with caveat: 'CIJ was reported as recording 36 Sedition Act cases in 2025 versus 19 in 2024; percentage increase should be checked against CIJ original report.'

claims.claim: Singapore's Maintenance of Religious Harmony Act came into force in 1992.
claims.source: Singapore Statutes Online, MRHA 1990. Act 26 of 1990, came into force 31 March 1992.

claims.claim: No restraining order under Singapore's Maintenance of Religious Harmony Act appears in the public record.
claims.source: Singapore Journal of Legal Studies article citing Singapore Parliamentary Debates and Minister Wong Kan Seng's 12 Feb 2007 answer; Straits Times search result, 7 Jan 2025, reporting authorities had never resorted to a restraining order under MRHA.
claims.correction: Careful phrasing 'appears in the public record' is acceptable. Most solid official disclosure is the 2007 parliamentary answer, with later secondary reporting indicating no subsequent use.
claims.stale_check: Could be stale if RO issued after latest checked secondary reporting; found no contrary record up to 2025 sources checked.

claims.claim: Zimbabwe's National Peace and Reconciliation Commission ran a 10-year mandate that ended in 2023.
claims.source: Zimbabwe Supreme Court judgment SC119-21; Zimbabwe Independent/NewsDay, 11 Sep 2023
claims.correction: The legal controversy is more complex. The Constitution created a commission with a limited term; Supreme Court set aside the High Court order that would have deemed term to run from 5 Jan 2018 to 2028. Reporting says term ended in 2023. 'Ran a 10-year mandate' is misleading because NPRC was not operational for the full ten years; the enabling Act came into force in 2018.
claims.stale_check: Should reflect 2021 Supreme Court ruling and 2018 operationalisation gap.

claims.claim: Independent reviews trace the failure to political will, not framework design.
claims.source: Zimbabwe Independent/NewsDay; NPRC Annual Report 2023; SSRC/Kujenga Amani
claims.correction: Political will is a documented criticism, but wording over-narrows the causal account. Sources also point to underfunding, delayed operationalisation, independence concerns, political interference, limited trust, and capacity constraints. Rephrase: 'Independent commentary identifies lack of political will as a major factor, alongside delayed operationalisation, underfunding, independence concerns and institutional constraints.'

claims.claim: After four drafts, the Harmony Bill exists. After 14 years, the Sedition Act repeal does not.
claims.source: The Star, 7 Apr 2026; The Star, 12 May 2026 'Claims against SKN misleading'
claims.correction: Sedition Act repeal not existing is verified. 'The Harmony Bill exists' is too strong because the 12 May 2026 ministry clarification says the proposal is still in engagement, must undergo AGC review, regulatory impact analysis, Cabinet approval and Dewan Rakyat tabling, and may be discontinued. Rephrase: 'After repeated drafting attempts, a new SKRN proposal is being revived; after 14 years, repeal of the Sedition Act has not occurred.'

claims.claim: Singapore's Act sits unused. Zimbabwe's commission delivered nothing.
claims.source: Singapore Journal of Legal Studies; NPRC Annual Report 2023
claims.correction: Singapore MRHA appears unused in narrow sense of formal restraining orders, but warnings and close calls are part of record. Zimbabwe NPRC did not deliver Gukurahundi closure, but annual report records complaints handling, mediation, community work. Over-compresses two different institutional records.

claims.claim: The Malaysian draft does not propose to repeal the Sedition Act.
claims.source: The Star, 7 Apr 2026; The Star 12 May 2026
claims.correction: No public draft text located. Public statements describe prevention, mediation, investigation, recommendations and reports, but do not state whether Sedition Act repeal is included or excluded. Rephrase: 'Public statements on the revived SKRN proposal do not mention Sedition Act repeal; no public draft text was found.'

cardinal_sin_flags.why: Actually a 15 February 2020 article about Waytha Moorthy. Material source attribution error.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Anwar pledged the same in 2018 and 2022.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: 2018 verified source is PH manifesto, not personal Anwar pledge.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: CIJ recorded 36 sedition cases in 2025, an 84% rise from the year before.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Primary CIJ 2025 report not located. 36 vs 19 produces about 89.5%, not 84%.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Zimbabwe's NPRC ran a 10-year mandate that ended in 2023.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Enabling Act came into force 2018; commission had ~5 operational years.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Independent reviews trace the failure to political will, not framework design.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Sources also cite underfunding, delayed operationalisation, independence concerns, resource constraints.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Singapore's Act sits unused. Zimbabwe's commission delivered nothing.
The Malaysian draft does not propose to repeal the Sedition Act.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No public 2026 draft text located. Omission is not proof of exclusion.

omitted_facts: The cited Bernama URL is actually a 15 February 2020 article about Waytha Moorthy.

12 May 2026 Ministry of National Unity clarification: engagement sessions are not final decision-making sessions, Bill must still go through ministry evaluation, AGC review, Regulatory Impact Analysis, Cabinet approval, Dewan Rakyat tabling, and may be discontinued: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/12/claims-against-skn-misleading
Same 12 May 2026 clarification says proposed commission will not alter fundamental constitutional provisions including Article 3, 121, 32, 38, 153 and 152.
Singapore MRHA has formal publication and procedural requirements; absence of publicly recorded ROs is meaningful but the public record also contains warnings and close-call episodes.
Zimbabwe NPRC lifespan was legally contested. High Court ordered ten-year term to be deemed to start in 2018; Supreme Court set that judgment aside.
NPRC 2023 Annual Report records activities, complaints handling and concluded investigations.
CIJ 36-versus-19 arithmetic does not equal 84% increase — simple calculation gives 89.5%.
source_assessment: Reasonably broad source mix. Comparator statutory claims well anchored. Singapore zero-RO claim cautiously phrased. CIJ 2025 numbers not supported by located primary CIJ report. Zimbabwe section blends official law, Supreme Court judgment, NPRC's own annual report and critical news commentary — compressed into stronger claims than the record supports.
overall_assessment: The central news event is substantially accurate: on 7 April 2026, Aaron Ago Dagang's ministry revived work on an SKRN/Harmony Commission proposal and named Singapore, Australia and Zimbabwe as comparators with a preventive and conciliatory framing. The Singapore statutory description is mostly accurate, and the Sedition Act remains in force. However, several publication-risk issues require correction before release: the Bernama URL is misdated and misattributed, the CIJ percentage appears mathematically wrong and lacks a located primary report, the 'Anwar pledged in 2018 and 2022' attribution is over-personalised, the Zimbabwe section over-compresses a contested legal and institutional record, and the claim that the Malaysian draft does not propose Sedition Act repeal is not verifiable without a public draft text.
